steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage describes Cumberland Mountain's pumped-storage facility: during low demand, water is pumped up to a summit lake; during peak demand, that water drains down and spins turbines to generate power. The question asks for the main purpose of the text.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "To point out the differences between two methods of energy generation.": The passage describes only one facility and one technology. No second method is presented for comparison. ✗
  • "To explain the basics of how a specific energy technology works.": The passage systematically walks through how Cumberland Mountain operates — pumping water up during low demand, releasing it to generate power during peak demand. This is an explanation of how the technology works. ✓
  • "To discuss the benefits of a new energy technology.": The passage mentions one capability (powering one million homes for twenty hours) but focuses on mechanism, not on promoting benefits. Also, Cumberland Mountain was built in the 1970s — it is not new. ✗
  • "To encourage regional electric utilities to build energy storage facilities.": The passage is purely descriptive; it makes no argument or call to action. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
